title: Pineapple fritters
subtitle: Battered pineapple rings, deep-fried and served warm
excerpt: Pineapple fritters are rings of fresh pineapple dipped in a light batter and deep-fried until crisp and golden. The batter is thin enough that the pineapple flavour comes through clearly. They take about 20 minutes from batter to table and are best eaten immediately.
sourceNotes: Pineapple fritters are a British Chinese takeaway and seaside tradition, adopted from Cantonese cooking where battered and fried fruit is a classic dessert technique. The batter method used here is a simple British beer-batter variant adapted for home use. This recipe is synthesised from the established British takeaway tradition.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The batter can be made up to an hour ahead and kept at room temperature. Fry to order.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: deep-fried at 180 degrees C, not oven-baked

glossaryTerms.definition: A good fritter batter clings to the fruit without running off but is not so thick it forms a heavy casing. The consistency should coat the back of a spoon and drip off in a slow, steady stream. Too thick: add cold water a tablespoon at a time. Too thin: add a tablespoon of flour and stir briefly. Resting the batter for 10 minutes before using helps the flour hydrate fully.

content.text: here uses cold sparkling water or plain water. The cold temperature and the carbonation both contribute to a lighter crust. Dry the pineapple rings thoroughly before dipping; any surface moisture will cause the batter to slip off.
Deep-frying: Keep the oil temperature at 180 degrees C and use a probe thermometer. Never leave hot oil unattended. Fry in batches of 3 or 4 to prevent the oil temperature dropping.

items.prepNote: peeled and cored, cut into 1.5 cm rings

content.text: Whisk together the 150 g flour, 1 tsp baking powder, a pinch of salt and 2 tsp caster sugar in a bowl. Add 180 ml cold water gradually, whisking until a smooth, pourable batter forms. It should coat the back of a spoon. Leave to rest for 10 minutes. Pat the pineapple rings dry with kitchen paper.

Heat the oil to 180 degrees C. Working in batches of 3 to 4, dip each pineapple ring into the batter, letting the excess drip off, then lower carefully into the hot oil. Fry for 2 to 3 minutes per side until the batter is a deep golden brown and crisp. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain briefly on kitchen paper. Sprinkle with caster sugar and serve immediately.

items.solution: The pineapple was not dried thoroughly. Pat each ring dry with kitchen paper until no surface moisture remains. Optionally, dust lightly with a little flour before dipping in the batter to give it something to grip.
The oil was below 180 degrees C. Cool oil makes the batter absorb oil rather than sealing quickly. Use a thermometer and let the oil recover fully between batches.
